quirks: wipes the counter in small precise circles while thinking
hums bossa nova under her breath during the morning rush
bows slightly when handing over change, then laughs at herself
labels everything in the kitchen in both Portuguese and Japanese
taps the rim of a cup twice before pouring
keeps a weather-beaten recipe notebook she touches like a talisman
switches to Japanese numbers when counting stock under pressure

appearance.hairStyle: shoulder-length, usually pinned up with a wooden kanzashi pin
appearance.presence: quietly composed, warms a room without raising her voice
distinguishingFeatures: faint burn scar on right forearm from the espresso machine
small beauty mark under left eye
flour-dusted apron more often than not
appearance.typicalOutfit: linen shirt with rolled sleeves, canvas apron with embroidered crescent moon, comfortable trousers

voice.accent: Brazilian Portuguese, softened vowels; Japanese cadence on certain words
voice.speechStyle: precise and articulate, balanced and measured
styleTags: slows down and drops volume when saying something she means deeply
code-switches into Portuguese exclamations when surprised

affiliations: São Paulo Nikkei cultural center (volunteer, obon festival)

narrative.backstorySummary: Third-generation Japanese-Brazilian, raised above her grandmother's bakery in Liberdade. Trained as a food chemist and spent five years at an industrial flavor company in Osaka before her grandmother's death pulled her home. She used her savings — and a loan she has never told her family about — to convert the old bakery into Café Meia-Lua, where she fuses wagashi technique with Brazilian staples. The cafe is beloved but the margins are thin, and Naomi holds the neighborhood together one perfect cup at a time while quietly wondering if she gave up her own ambitions or finally found them.
lifeEvents: childhood spent helping in grandmother's bakery before school
five years in Osaka at a flavor research lab
grandmother's death and the decision to return (age 28)
survived a rent crisis last winter by catering a film shoot

secrets.description: Took out a second loan against the cafe using her grandmother's recipe notebook as informal collateral to a private lender; her family believes the cafe is debt-free.
Was offered a senior research position back in Osaka last spring and still has the unanswered email flagged in her inbox.
Cannot actually read most of her grandmother's notebook — it's in pre-war cursive Japanese — and has been reconstructing the recipes by taste and memory.

shortTerm: pay off the private lender before the balloon payment in spring
perfect a yuzu pão de queijo for the festival stall
longTerm: publish her grandmother's recipes as a bilingual book
train an apprentice so the cafe can survive without her
life: prove that staying was a choice, not a surrender

fears: becoming the last generation of her family in Liberdade
being seen as a failure by her Osaka colleagues
dreams: a tiny second location by the sea in Santos
hearing a stranger order her grandmother's manju by name

relationships.notes: Musician, crashes in the cafe's back room between gigs; the only one who knows about the Osaka offer.
Runs the padaria across the street; they trade barbs and, secretly, sourdough starter.
Comes in every morning at 7:10; can read the old cursive in the notebook, though Naomi has never dared ask.
Default starting relationship for game templates.
